tEn: Emergent Abilities of Large Language Models
tZh: 大型语言模型的涌现能力
sEn: Emergent abilities are unpredictable capabilities that appear only in large language models, not in smaller ones.
sZh: 涌现能力是指仅在大型语言模型中出现、而在较小模型中不存在的不可预测的能力。
absEn: Scaling up language models has been shown to predictably improve performance and sample efficiency on a wide range of downstream tasks. This paper instead discusses an unpredictable phenomenon that we refer to as emergent abilities of large language models. We consider an ability to be emergent if it is not present in smaller models but is present in larger models. Thus, emergent abilities cannot be predicted simply by extrapolating the performance of smaller models. The existence of such emergence implies that additional scaling could further expand the range of capabilities of language models.
absZh: 扩展语言模型已被证明可以可预测地提高在广泛下游任务上的性能和样本效率。然而，本文讨论了一种不可预测的现象，我们称之为大型语言模型的涌现能力。我们认为，如果一种能力在较小模型中不存在，但在较大模型中出现，那么它就是涌现的。因此，涌现能力不能简单地通过外推较小模型的性能来预测。这种涌现的存在意味着进一步扩展可能会扩大语言模型的能力范围。

contrib.en: Defines emergent abilities as abilities present in large models but absent in smaller ones, unpredictable via scaling laws.
contrib.zh: 将涌现能力定义为大模型具备而小模型不具备的能力，无法通过缩放定律预测。

contrib.en: Documents emergent abilities across few-shot prompting, chain-of-thought, instruction following, and program execution.
contrib.zh: 记录了少样本提示、思维链、指令遵循和程序执行等场景中的涌现能力。

contrib.en: Shows emergent abilities appear as phase transitions in scaling curves, with performance jumping from random to above-random at a threshold.
contrib.zh: 展示涌现能力在缩放曲线中表现为相变，性能在阈值处从随机跃升至显著高于随机。

contrib.en: Discusses that emergent abilities are not solely determined by scale; architecture, data quality, and training procedures also matter.
contrib.zh: 讨论涌现能力并非仅由规模决定；架构、数据质量和训练过程同样重要。

contrib.en: Highlights emergent risks such as bias, toxicity, and memorization that can increase with scale.
contrib.zh: 强调随规模增加而涌现的风险，如偏见、毒性和记忆化。

contrib.en: Proposes future directions including further scaling, improved architectures, data scaling, and understanding emergence.
contrib.zh: 提出未来方向，包括进一步扩展、改进架构、数据扩展和理解涌现。

limits.en: Emergent abilities are defined only for pre-trained Transformer language models, not other architectures.
limits.zh: 涌现能力仅针对预训练Transformer语言模型定义，不适用于其他架构。

limits.en: The paper does not propose new methods or models; it surveys existing results.
limits.zh: 本文未提出新方法或模型，仅综述现有结果。

limits.en: Emergence may be an artifact of evaluation metrics like exact match, which ignore partial progress.
limits.zh: 涌现可能是评估指标（如精确匹配）的假象，忽略了部分进展。

limits.en: The scale at which emergence occurs is not fixed; it depends on data quality, architecture, and training.
limits.zh: 涌现发生的规模并非固定，取决于数据质量、架构和训练。

limits.en: Some abilities may never emerge due to distributional mismatch or plateauing after emergence.
limits.zh: 某些能力可能因分布不匹配或涌现后停滞而永远不会出现。

items.en: Scaling up language models has been shown to predictably improve performance and sample efficiency on a wide range of downstream tasks. This paper instead discusses an unpredictable phenomenon that we refer to as emergent abilities of large language models. We consider an ability to be emergent if it is not present in smaller models but is present in larger models. Thus, emergent abilities cannot be predicted simply by extrapolating the performance of smaller models. The existence of such emergence raises the question of whether additional scaling could potentially further expand the range of capabilities of language models.
items.zh: 扩展语言模型已被证明可预测地提高广泛下游任务的性能和样本效率。本文讨论的是一种不可预测的现象，我们称之为大型语言模型的涌现能力。如果某种能力在较小模型中不存在，但在较大模型中出现，我们就认为它是涌现的。因此，涌现能力无法通过简单外推较小模型的性能来预测。这种涌现的存在提出了一个问题：进一步的扩展是否可能进一步扩大语言模型的能力范围。

items.en: Language models have revolutionized natural language processing (NLP) in recent years. It is now well-known that increasing the scale of language models (e.g., training compute, model parameters, etc.) can lead to better performance and sample efficiency on a range of downstream NLP tasks (Devlin et al., 2019; Brown et al., 2020, inter alia). In many cases, the effect of scale on performance can often be methodologically predicted via scaling laws—for example, scaling curves for cross-entropy loss have been shown to empirically span more than seven orders of magnitude (Kaplan et al., 2020; Hoffmann et al., 2022). On the other hand, performance for certain downstream tasks counterintuitively does not appear to continuously improve as a function of scale, and such tasks cannot be predicted ahead of time (Ganguli et al., 2022).
items.zh: 近年来，语言模型彻底改变了自然语言处理（NLP）。众所周知，增加语言模型的规模（例如，训练算力、模型参数等）可以在一系列下游NLP任务上带来更好的性能和样本效率（Devlin等人，2019；Brown等人，2020等）。在许多情况下，规模对性能的影响通常可以通过缩放定律进行方法论上的预测——例如，交叉熵损失的缩放曲线已被经验证明跨越超过七个数量级（Kaplan等人，2020；Hoffmann等人，2022）。另一方面，某些下游任务的性能反直觉地似乎并不随规模连续改善，并且这些任务无法提前预测（Ganguli等人，2022）。

items.en: In this paper, we will discuss the unpredictable phenomena of emergent abilities of large language models. Emergence as an idea has been long discussed in domains such as physics, biology, and computer science (Anderson, 1972; Hwang et al., 2012; Forrest, 1990; Corradini & O’Connor, 2010; Harper & Lewis, 2012, inter alia). We will consider the following general definition of emergence, adapted from Steinhardt (2022) and rooted in a 1972 essay called “More Is Different” by Nobel prize-winning physicist Philip Anderson (Anderson, 1972):
items.zh: 在本文中，我们将讨论大型语言模型涌现能力的不可预测现象。涌现作为一个概念，在物理学、生物学和计算机科学等领域已被长期讨论（Anderson，1972；Hwang等人，2012；Forrest，1990；Corradini & O'Connor，2010；Harper & Lewis，2012等）。我们将考虑以下涌现的一般定义，该定义改编自Steinhardt（2022），并源于诺贝尔奖得主物理学家Philip Anderson在1972年的一篇题为“More Is Different”的文章（Anderson，1972）：

items.en: Emergence is when quantitative changes in a system result in qualitative changes in behavior.
items.zh: 涌现是指系统中量的变化导致行为质的变化。

items.en: Here we will explore emergence with respect to model scale, as measured by training compute and number of model parameters. Specifically, we define emergent abilities of large language models as abilities that are not present in smaller-scale models but are present in large-scale models; thus they cannot be predicted by simply extrapolating the performance improvements on smaller-scale models (§ 2).111This survey focuses on pre-trained Transformer language models. Emergent abilities in NLP more broadly, however, could go back to Miller et al. (2004), Liang (2005), or earlier. We survey emergent abilities as observed in a range of prior work, categorizing them in settings such as few-shot prompting (§ 3) and augmented prompting strategies (§ 4). Emergence motivates future research on why such abilities are acquired and whether more scaling will lead to further emergent abilities, which we highlight as important questions for the field (§ 5).
items.zh: 在这里，我们将探讨与模型规模相关的涌现，规模通过训练算力和模型参数数量来衡量。具体来说，我们将大型语言模型的涌现能力定义为在较小规模模型中不存在但在大规模模型中存在的能力；因此，它们无法通过简单外推较小规模模型的性能改进来预测（§2）。111本综述聚焦于预训练的Transformer语言模型。然而，NLP中更广泛的涌现能力可以追溯到Miller等人（2004）、Liang（2005）或更早。我们调查了先前工作中观察到的涌现能力，将其分类为少样本提示（§3）和增强提示策略（§4）等场景。涌现激发了未来研究：为什么这些能力会被获得，以及更多的扩展是否会导致进一步的涌现能力，我们将其强调为该领域的重要问题（§5）。

items.en: As a broad concept, emergence is often used informally and can be reasonably interpreted in many different ways. In this paper, we will consider a focused definition of emergent abilities of large language models:
items.zh: 作为一个宽泛的概念，涌现通常被非正式地使用，并且可以有多种合理的解释。在本文中，我们将考虑大型语言模型涌现能力的一个聚焦定义：

items.en: An ability is emergent if it is not present in smaller models but is present in larger models.
items.zh: 如果某种能力在较小模型中不存在，但在较大模型中出现，则该能力是涌现的。

items.en: Emergent abilities would not have been directly predicted by extrapolating a scaling law (i.e. consistent performance improvements) from small-scale models. When visualized via a scaling curve (x𝑥x-axis: model scale, y𝑦y-axis: performance), emergent abilities show a clear pattern—performance is near-random until a certain critical threshold of scale is reached, after which performance increases to substantially above random. This qualitative change is also known as a phase transition—a dramatic change in overall behavior that would not have been foreseen by examining smaller-scale systems (Huberman & Hogg, 1987).
items.zh: 涌现能力无法通过从小规模模型外推缩放定律（即一致的性能改进）直接预测。当通过缩放曲线（x轴：模型规模，y轴：性能）可视化时，涌现能力显示出清晰的模式——性能在达到某个临界规模阈值之前接近随机，之后性能显著提升至远高于随机。这种质变也称为相变——一种整体行为的剧烈变化，无法通过检查较小规模系统预见（Huberman & Hogg，1987）。

items.en: Today’s language models have been scaled primarily along three factors: amount of computation, number of model parameters, and training dataset size (Kaplan et al., 2020; Hoffmann et al., 2022). In this paper, we will analyze scaling curves by plotting the performance of different models where training compute for each model is measured in FLOPs on the x𝑥x-axis (Hoffmann et al., 2022). Because language models trained with more compute tend to also have more parameters, we additionally show plots with number of model parameters as the x𝑥x-axis in Appendix D (see Figure 11 and Figure 12, as well as Figure 4 and Figure 10). Using training FLOPs or model parameters as the x𝑥x-axis produces curves with similar shapes due to the fact that most dense Transformer language model families have scaled training compute roughly proportionally with model parameters (Kaplan et al., 2020).
items.zh: 当今的语言模型主要沿着三个因素进行扩展：计算量、模型参数数量和训练数据集大小（Kaplan等人，2020；Hoffmann等人，2022）。在本文中，我们将通过绘制不同模型的性能曲线来分析缩放曲线，其中每个模型的训练算力以FLOPs为单位在x轴上表示（Hoffmann等人，2022）。由于训练算力更大的语言模型通常也有更多参数，我们还在附录D中展示了以模型参数数量为x轴的图（见图11和图12，以及图4和图10）。使用训练FLOPs或模型参数作为x轴会产生形状相似的曲线，这是因为大多数密集Transformer语言模型家族的训练算力大致与模型参数成比例扩展（Kaplan等人，2020）。

items.en: Training dataset size is also an important factor, but we do not plot capabilities against it because many language model families use a fixed number of training examples for all model sizes (Brown et al., 2020; Rae et al., 2021; Chowdhery et al., 2022). Although we focus on training computation and model size here, there is not a single proxy that adequately captures all aspects of scale. For example, Chinchilla (Hoffmann et al., 2022) has one-fourth as many parameters as Gopher (Rae et al., 2021) but uses similar training compute; and sparse mixture-of-expert models have more parameters per training/inference compute than dense models (Fedus et al., 2021; Du et al., 2021). Overall, it may be wise to view emergence as a function of many correlated variables. For example, later in Figure 4 we will also plot emergence as a function of WikiText103 perplexity (Merity et al., 2016), which happens to closely correlate with training computation for Gopher/ Chinchilla (though this correlation may not hold in the long-run).
items.zh: 训练数据集大小也是一个重要因素，但我们不针对它绘制能力曲线，因为许多语言模型家族对所有模型大小使用固定数量的训练样本（Brown等人，2020；Rae等人，2021；Chowdhery等人，2022）。尽管我们在这里关注训练计算和模型大小，但没有任何单一的代理变量能充分捕捉规模的所有方面。例如，Chinchilla（Hoffmann等人，2022）的参数数量是Gopher（Rae等人，2021）的四分之一，但使用了相似的训练算力；而稀疏混合专家模型每个训练/推理算力的参数比密集模型更多（Fedus等人，2021；Du等人，2021）。总体而言，将涌现视为许多相关变量的函数可能是明智的。例如，稍后在图4中，我们还将绘制涌现作为WikiText103困惑度（Merity等人，2016）的函数，该困惑度恰好与Gopher/Chinchilla的训练计算密切相关（尽管这种相关性可能不会长期保持）。

items.en: Note that the scale at which an ability is first observed to emerge depends on a number of factors and is not an immutable property of the ability. For instance, emergence may occur with less training compute or fewer model parameters for models trained on higher-quality data. Conversely, emergent abilities also crucially depend on other factors such as not being limited by the amount of data, its quality, or the number of parameters in the model. Today’s language models are likely not trained optimally (Hoffmann et al., 2022), and our understanding of how to best train models will evolve over time. Our goal in this paper is not to characterize or claim that a specific scale is required to observe emergent abilities, but rather, we aim to discuss examples of emergent behavior in prior work.
items.zh: 请注意，首次观察到能力涌现的规模取决于许多因素，并且不是该能力的固定属性。例如，对于在更高质量数据上训练的模型，涌现可能发生在更少的训练算力或更少的模型参数下。相反，涌现能力也关键地依赖于其他因素，例如不受数据量、数据质量或模型参数数量的限制。当今的语言模型可能没有以最优方式训练（Hoffmann等人，2022），我们对如何最佳训练模型的理解将随时间演变。本文的目标不是描述或声称需要特定规模才能观察到涌现能力，而是旨在讨论先前工作中涌现行为的例子。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Example of an input and output for few-shot prompting.
items.capZh: 图1：少样本提示的输入和输出示例。

items.en: We first discuss emergent abilities in the prompting paradigm, as popularized by GPT-3 (Brown et al., 2020).222Though GPT-3 popularized prompting, the task setup has existed since before GPT-3 (Trinh & Le, 2018; McCann et al., 2018; Radford et al., 2019; Raffel et al., 2020). In prompting, a pre-trained language model is given a prompt (e.g. a natural language instruction) of a task and completes the response without any further training or gradient updates to its parameters. Brown et al. (2020) proposed few-shot prompting, which includes a few input-output examples in the model’s context (input) as a preamble before asking the model to perform the task for an unseen inference-time example. An example prompt is shown in Figure 1.
items.zh: 我们首先讨论提示范式中的涌现能力，该范式由GPT-3推广（Brown等人，2020）。222尽管GPT-3推广了提示，但任务设置早在GPT-3之前就已存在（Trinh & Le，2018；McCann等人，2018；Radford等人，2019；Raffel等人，2020）。在提示中，预训练语言模型被给予一个任务提示（例如，自然语言指令），并在不进行任何进一步训练或对其参数进行梯度更新的情况下完成响应。Brown等人（2020）提出了少样本提示，它在模型的上下文（输入）中包含几个输入-输出示例作为前导，然后要求模型对未见过的推理时示例执行任务。图1显示了一个示例提示。

items.en: The ability to perform a task via few-shot prompting is emergent when a model has random performance until a certain scale, after which performance increases to well-above random. Figure 2 shows eight such emergent abilities spanning five language model families from various work.
items.zh: 当模型在达到一定规模之前性能随机，之后性能提升至远高于随机时，通过少样本提示执行任务的能力就是涌现的。图2显示了来自各种工作的五个语言模型家族的八种这样的涌现能力。

items.en: BIG-Bench. Figure 2A–D depicts four emergent few-shot prompted tasks from BIG-Bench, a crowd-sourced suite of over 200 benchmarks for language model evaluation (BIG-Bench, 2022). Figure 2A shows an arithmetic benchmark that tests 3-digit addition and subtraction, as well as 2-digit multiplication. GPT-3 and LaMDA (Thoppilan et al., 2022) have close-to-zero performance for several orders of magnitude of training compute, before performance jumps to sharply above random at 2⋅1022⋅2superscript10222\cdot 10^{22} training FLOPs (13B parameters) for GPT-3, and 1023superscript102310^{23} training FLOPs (68B parameters) for LaMDA. Similar emergent behavior also occurs at around the same model scale for other tasks, such as transliterating from the International Phonetic Alphabet (Figure 2B), recovering a word from its scrambled letters (Figure 2C), and Persian question-answering (Figure 2D). Even more emergent abilities from BIG-Bench are given in Appendix E.
items.zh: BIG-Bench。图2A–D展示了来自BIG-Bench的四个涌现的少样本提示任务，BIG-Bench是一个众包的包含200多个语言模型评估基准的套件（BIG-Bench，2022）。图2A显示了一个算术基准，测试三位数加减法和两位数乘法。GPT-3和LaMDA（Thoppilan等人，2022）在几个数量级的训练算力范围内性能接近零，然后在2·10^22训练FLOPs（13B参数）时GPT-3的性能跃升至远高于随机，LaMDA在10^23训练FLOPs（68B参数）时出现类似情况。其他任务也在大约相同的模型规模下出现类似的涌现行为，例如从国际音标音译（图2B）、从乱序字母中恢复单词（图2C）和波斯语问答（图2D）。BIG-Bench中更多的涌现能力见附录E。

items.en: TruthfulQA. Figure 2E shows few-shot prompted performance on the TruthfulQA benchmark, which measures the ability to answer questions truthfully (Lin et al., 2021). This benchmark is adversarially curated against GPT-3 models, which do not perform above random, even when scaled to the largest model size. Small Gopher models also do not perform above random until scaled up to the largest model of 5⋅1023⋅5superscript10235\cdot 10^{23} training FLOPs (280B parameters), for which performance jumps to more than 20% above random (Rae et al., 2021).
items.zh: TruthfulQA。图2E显示了TruthfulQA基准上的少样本提示性能，该基准衡量真实回答问题的能力（Lin等人，2021）。该基准是针对GPT-3模型对抗性策划的，即使扩展到最大模型大小，GPT-3模型的表现也不高于随机。小型Gopher模型在扩展到最大模型5·10^23训练FLOPs（280B参数）之前也不高于随机，此时性能跃升至比随机高20%以上（Rae等人，2021）。

items.en: Grounded conceptual mappings. Figure 2F shows the task of grounded conceptual mappings, where language models must learn to map a conceptual domain, such as a cardinal direction, represented in a textual grid world (Patel & Pavlick, 2022). Again, performance only jumps to above random using the largest GPT-3 model.
items.zh: 基础概念映射。图2F显示了基础概念映射任务，其中语言模型必须学习映射一个概念领域，例如文本网格世界中表示的基本方向（Patel & Pavlick，2022）。同样，只有使用最大的GPT-3模型，性能才跃升至高于随机。

items.en: Multi-task language understanding. Figure 2G shows the Massive Multi-task Language Understanding (MMLU) benchmark, which aggregates 57 tests covering a range of topics including math, history, law, and more (Hendrycks et al., 2021a). For GPT-3, Gopher, and Chinchilla, models of ∼1022similar-toabsentsuperscript1022{\sim}10^{22} training FLOPs (∼similar-to\sim10B parameters) or smaller do not perform better than guessing on average over all the topics, scaling up to 333–555 ⋅1023⋅absentsuperscript1023\cdot 10^{23} training FLOPs (70B–280B parameters) enables performance to substantially surpass random. This result is striking because it could imply that the ability to solve knowledge-based questions spanning a large collection of topics might require scaling up past this threshold (for dense language models without retrieval or access to external memory).
items.zh: 多任务语言理解。图2G显示了大规模多任务语言理解（MMLU）基准，该基准汇总了57个测试，涵盖数学、历史、法律等主题（Hendrycks等人，2021a）。对于GPT-3、Gopher和Chinchilla，训练算力约10^22 FLOPs（约10B参数）或更小的模型在所有主题上的平均表现不优于随机猜测，而扩展到3–5·10^23训练FLOPs（70B–280B参数）使性能显著超过随机。这一结果引人注目，因为它可能意味着解决跨越大量主题的知识型问题的能力可能需要扩展超过这个阈值（对于没有检索或外部记忆的密集语言模型）。

items.en: Word in Context. Finally, Figure 2H shows the Word in Context (WiC) benchmark (Pilehvar & Camacho-Collados, 2019), which is a semantic understanding benchmark. Notably, GPT-3 and Chinchilla fail to achieve one-shot performance of better than random, even when scaled to their largest model size of ∼5⋅1023similar-toabsent⋅5superscript1023{\sim}5\cdot 10^{23} FLOPs. Although these results so far may suggest that scaling alone may not enable models to solve WiC, above-random performance eventually emerged when PaLM was scaled to 2.5⋅1024⋅2.5superscript10242.5\cdot 10^{24} FLOPs (540B parameters), which was much larger than GPT-3 and Chinchilla.
items.zh: Word in Context。最后，图2H显示了Word in Context（WiC）基准（Pilehvar & Camacho-Collados，2019），这是一个语义理解基准。值得注意的是，GPT-3和Chinchilla即使在扩展到最大模型大小约5·10^23 FLOPs时，也未能实现优于随机的一次性性能。尽管这些结果可能表明仅靠扩展可能无法使模型解决WiC，但当PaLM扩展到2.5·10^24 FLOPs（540B参数）时，最终出现了高于随机的性能，这远大于GPT-3和Chinchilla。

items.en: Although few-shot prompting is perhaps currently the most common way of interacting with large language models, recent work has proposed several other prompting and finetuning strategies to further augment the abilities of language models. If a technique shows no improvement or is harmful when compared to the baseline of not using the technique until applied to a model of a large-enough scale, we also consider the technique an emergent ability.
items.zh: 尽管少样本提示可能是目前与大型语言模型交互的最常见方式，但最近的工作提出了几种其他提示和微调策略，以进一步增强语言模型的能力。如果一种技术在不使用该技术的基线上没有改进或有害，直到应用于足够大规模的模型时才有效，我们也认为该技术是一种涌现能力。

items.en: Multi-step reasoning. Reasoning tasks, especially those involving multiple steps, have been challenging for language models and NLP models more broadly (Rae et al., 2021; Bommasani et al., 2021; Nye et al., 2021). A recent prompting strategy called chain-of-thought prompting enables language models to solve such problems by guiding them to produce a sequence of intermediate steps before giving the final answer (Cobbe et al., 2021; Wei et al., 2022b; Suzgun et al., 2022). As shown in Figure 3A, chain of thought prompting only surpasses standard prompting without intermediate steps when scaled to 1023superscript102310^{23} training FLOPs (∼similar-to\sim100B parameters). A similar emergence in performance gain was also observed when augmenting few-shot prompting with explanations that came after the final answer (Lampinen et al., 2022).
items.zh: 多步推理。推理任务，尤其是涉及多个步骤的任务，对语言模型和更广泛的NLP模型来说一直具有挑战性（Rae等人，2021；Bommasani等人，2021；Nye等人，2021）。最近一种称为思维链提示的策略通过引导语言模型在给出最终答案前生成一系列中间步骤，使它们能够解决此类问题（Cobbe等人，2021；Wei等人，2022b；Suzgun等人，2022）。如图3A所示，思维链提示仅在扩展到10^23训练FLOPs（约100B参数）时，才超越没有中间步骤的标准提示。在最终答案后添加解释的少样本提示中也观察到了类似的性能涌现（Lampinen等人，2022）。

items.en: Instruction following. Another growing line of work aims to better enable language models to perform new tasks simply by reading instructions describing the task (without few-shot exemplars). By finetuning on a mixture of tasks phrased as instructions, language models have been shown to respond appropriately to instructions describing an unseen task (Ouyang et al., 2022; Wei et al., 2022a; Sanh et al., 2022; Chung et al., 2022). As shown in Figure 3B, Wei et al. (2022a) found that this instruction-finetuning technique hurts performance for models of 7⋅1021⋅7superscript10217\cdot 10^{21} training FLOPs (8B parameters) or smaller, and only improves performance when scaled to 1023superscript102310^{23} training FLOPs (∼similar-to\sim100B parameters) (though Sanh et al. (2022) found shortly after that this instruction-following behavior could be also induced by finetuning smaller encoder-decoder T5 models).
items.zh: 指令遵循。另一项日益增长的工作旨在通过让语言模型阅读描述任务的指令（无需少样本示例）来更好地执行新任务。通过在混合了指令形式的任务上进行微调，语言模型已被证明能够对描述未见任务的指令做出适当响应（Ouyang等人，2022；Wei等人，2022a；Sanh等人，2022；Chung等人，2022）。如图3B所示，Wei等人（2022a）发现，这种指令微调技术会损害训练FLOPs为7·10^21（8B参数）或更小的模型的性能，并且仅在扩展到10^23训练FLOPs（约100B参数）时才提高性能（尽管Sanh等人（2022）随后发现，这种指令遵循行为也可以通过微调较小的编码器-解码器T5模型来诱导）。

items.en: Program execution. Consider computational tasks involving multiple steps, such as adding large numbers or executing computer programs. Nye et al. (2021) show that finetuning language models to predict intermediate outputs (“scratchpad”) enables them to successfully execute such multi-step computations. As shown in Figure 3C, on 8-digit addition, using a scratchpad only helps for models of ∼9⋅1019similar-toabsent⋅9superscript1019{\sim}9\cdot 10^{19} training FLOPs (40M parameters) or larger.
items.zh: 程序执行。考虑涉及多个步骤的计算任务，例如大数相加或执行计算机程序。Nye等人（2021）表明，微调语言模型以预测中间输出（“草稿纸”）使它们能够成功执行此类多步计算。如图3C所示，在8位数字加法中，使用草稿纸仅对训练FLOPs约为9·10^19（40M参数）或更大的模型有帮助。

items.en: Model calibration. Finally, an important direction for deployment of language models studies is calibration, which measures whether models can predict which questions they will be able to answer correctly. Kadavath et al. (2022) compared two ways of measuring calibration: a True/False technique, where models first propose answers and then evaluate the probability “P(True)” that their answers are correct, and more-standard methods of calibration, which use the probability of the correct answer compared with other answer options. As shown in Figure 3D, the superiority of the True/False technique only emerges when scaled to the largest model scale of ∼3⋅1023similar-toabsent⋅3superscript1023{\sim}3\cdot 10^{23} training FLOPs (52B parameters).
items.zh: 模型校准。最后，语言模型部署研究的一个重要方向是校准，它衡量模型是否能预测哪些问题它们能正确回答。Kadavath等人（2022）比较了两种校准测量方法：真/假技术，即模型先提出答案，然后评估其答案正确的概率“P(True)”；以及更标准的校准方法，即使用正确答案与其他选项的概率比较。如图3D所示，真/假技术的优越性仅在扩展到最大模型规模（约3·10^23训练FLOPs，52B参数）时才涌现。

items.en: We have seen that a range of abilities—in the few-shot prompting setup or otherwise—have thus far only been observed when evaluated on a sufficiently large language model. Hence, their emergence cannot be predicted by simply extrapolating performance on smaller-scale models. Emergent few-shot prompted tasks are also unpredictable in the sense that these tasks are not explicitly included in pre-training, and we likely do not know the full scope of few-shot prompted tasks that language models can perform. This raises the question of whether further scaling could potentially endow even-larger language models with new emergent abilities. Tasks that language models cannot currently do are prime candidates for future emergence; for instance, there are dozens of tasks in BIG-Bench for which even the largest GPT-3 and PaLM models do not achieve above-random performance (see § E.4).
items.zh: 我们已经看到，一系列能力——无论是在少样本提示设置中还是其他情况下——迄今为止仅在足够大的语言模型上评估时才被观察到。因此，它们的涌现无法通过简单外推较小规模模型的性能来预测。涌现的少样本提示任务也是不可预测的，因为这些任务并未明确包含在预训练中，而且我们可能不知道语言模型能够执行的少样本提示任务的全部范围。这提出了一个问题：进一步扩展是否可能赋予更大的语言模型新的涌现能力。语言模型当前无法完成的任务是未来涌现的主要候选；例如，在BIG-Bench中有数十个任务，即使最大的GPT-3和PaLM模型也无法达到高于随机的性能（参见§ E.4）。

items.en: The ability for scale to unpredictably enable new techniques is not just theoretical. Consider the Word in Context (WiC) benchmark (Pilehvar & Camacho-Collados, 2019) shown in Figure 2H, as a historical example. Here, scaling GPT-3 to around 3⋅1023⋅3superscript10233\cdot 10^{23} training FLOPs (175B parameters) failed to unlock above-random one-shot prompting performance.333GPT-3 does achieve slightly above-random performance on the dev set with few-shot instead of one-shot prompting (∼similar-to\sim55%), but this above-random performance did not appear to be a result of scale and did not hold on the test set server. Regarding this negative result, Brown et al. (2020) cited the model architecture of GPT-3 or the use of an autoregressive language modeling objective (rather than using a denoising training objective) as potential reasons, and suggested training a model of comparable size with bidirectional architecture as a remedy. However, later work found that further scaling a decoder-only language model was actually enough to enable above-random performance on this task. As is shown in Figure 2H, scaling PaLM (Chowdhery et al., 2022) from 3⋅1023⋅3superscript10233\cdot 10^{23} training FLOPs (62B parameters) to 3⋅1024⋅3superscript10243\cdot 10^{24} training FLOPs (540B parameters) led to a significant jump in performance, without the significant architectural changes suggested by Brown et al. (2020).
items.zh: 规模能不可预测地启用新技术的能力不仅仅是理论上的。考虑图2H中所示的Word in Context（WiC）基准（Pilehvar & Camacho-Collados，2019）作为一个历史例子。在这里，将GPT-3扩展到约3·10^23训练FLOPs（175B参数）未能解锁高于随机的一次性提示性能。关于这个负面结果，Brown等人（2020）将原因归咎于GPT-3的模型架构或使用自回归语言建模目标（而不是使用去噪训练目标），并建议训练一个具有双向架构的类似大小的模型作为补救。然而，后来的工作发现，进一步扩展仅解码器语言模型实际上足以在该任务上实现高于随机的性能。如图2H所示，将PaLM（Chowdhery等人，2022）从3·10^23训练FLOPs（62B参数）扩展到3·10^24训练FLOPs（540B参数）导致了性能的显著跃升，而无需Brown等人（2020）建议的重大架构变化。

items.en: Although there are dozens of examples of emergent abilities, there are currently few compelling explanations for why such abilities emerge in the way they do. For certain tasks, there may be natural intuitions for why emergence requires a model larger than a particular threshold scale. For instance, if a multi-step reasoning task requires l𝑙l steps of sequential computation, this might require a model with a depth of at least O​(l)𝑂𝑙O\left(l\right) layers. It is also reasonable to assume that more parameters and more training enable better memorization that could be helpful for tasks requiring world knowledge.444Though note that encoding world knowledge in parameters is just one approach; there are others (e.g., Guu et al., 2020; Borgeaud et al., 2021). As an example, good performance on closed-book question-answering may require a model with enough parameters to capture the compressed knowledge base itself (though language model-based compressors can have higher compression ratios than conventional compressors (Bellard, 2021)).
items.zh: 尽管有数十个涌现能力的例子，但目前很少有令人信服的解释说明为什么这些能力会以这种方式涌现。对于某些任务，可能有自然的直觉解释为什么涌现需要大于特定阈值规模的模型。例如，如果一个多步推理任务需要l步顺序计算，这可能需要一个深度至少为O(l)层的模型。同样可以合理地假设，更多的参数和更多的训练能够实现更好的记忆，这可能有助于需要世界知识的任务。作为一个例子，闭卷问答的良好性能可能需要一个具有足够参数的模型来捕获压缩的知识库本身（尽管基于语言模型的压缩器可以比传统压缩器具有更高的压缩比（Bellard，2021））。

items.en: It is also important to consider the evaluation metrics used to measure emergent abilities (BIG-Bench, 2022). For instance, using exact string match as the evaluation metric for long-sequence targets may disguise compounding incremental improvements as emergence. Similar logic may apply for multi-step or arithmetic reasoning problems, where models are only scored on whether they get the final answer to a multi-step problem correct, without any credit given to partially correct solutions. However, the jump in final answer accuracy does not explain why the quality of intermediate steps suddenly emerges to above random, and using evaluation metrics that do not give partial credit are at best an incomplete explanation, because emergent abilities are still observed on many classification tasks (e.g., the tasks in Figure 2D–H).
items.zh: 考虑用于衡量涌现能力的评估指标也很重要（BIG-Bench，2022）。例如，对长序列目标使用精确字符串匹配作为评估指标可能会将复合的增量改进伪装成涌现。类似的逻辑可能适用于多步或算术推理问题，其中模型仅根据是否得到多步问题的最终正确答案来评分，而不对部分正确的解决方案给予任何分数。然而，最终答案准确率的跃升并不能解释为什么中间步骤的质量突然涌现到高于随机水平，而使用不给部分分数的评估指标充其量只是一个不完整的解释，因为在许多分类任务上仍然观察到涌现能力（例如，图2D–H中的任务）。

items.en: As an alternative evaluation, we measure cross-entropy loss, which is used in scaling laws for pre-training, for the six emergent BIG-Bench tasks, as detailed in Appendix A. This analysis follows the same experimental setup from BIG-Bench (2022) and affirms their conclusions for the six emergent tasks we consider. Namely, cross-entropy loss improves even for small model scales where the downstream metrics (exact match, BLEU, and accuracy) are close to random and do not improve, which shows that improvements in the log-likelihood of the target sequence can be masked by such downstream metrics. However, this analysis does not explain why downstream metrics are emergent or enable us to predict the scale at which emergence occurs. Overall, more work is needed to tease apart what enables scale to unlock emergent abilities.
items.zh: 作为替代评估，我们测量了交叉熵损失，它用于预训练的缩放定律，针对六个涌现的BIG-Bench任务，详见附录A。该分析遵循BIG-Bench（2022）的相同实验设置，并确认了他们对我们考虑的六个涌现任务的结论。也就是说，交叉熵损失即使在较小模型规模下也有所改善，而下游指标（精确匹配、BLEU和准确率）接近随机且没有改善，这表明目标序列对数似然的改进可能被这些下游指标掩盖。然而，该分析并未解释为什么下游指标是涌现的，也没有使我们能够预测涌现发生的规模。总体而言，需要更多的工作来厘清是什么使规模能够解锁涌现能力。

items.en: Although we may observe an emergent ability to occur at a certain scale, it is possible that the ability could be later achieved at a smaller scale—in other words, model scale is not the singular factor for unlocking an emergent ability. As the science of training large language models progresses, certain abilities may be unlocked for smaller models with new architectures, higher-quality data, or improved training procedures. For example, there are 14 BIG-Bench tasks555These tasks are enumerated in Appendix F. for which LaMDA 137B and GPT-3 175B models perform at near-random, but PaLM 62B in fact achieves above-random performance, despite having fewer model parameters and training FLOPs. While there is not an empirical study ablating every difference between PaLM 62B and prior models (the computational cost would be too high), potential reasons for the better performance of PaLM could include high-quality training data (e.g., more multilingual and code data than LaMDA) and architectural differences (e.g., split digit-encodings; see Section 2 in Chowdhery et al. (2022)). Another potentially way of unlocking emergence is through a different pre-training objective—it was shown in Tay et al. (2022c) that a computationally-efficient continued pre-training stage on a mixture-of-denoisers objective (Tay et al., 2022a) enabled emergent performance on several BIG-Bench tasks.
items.zh: 尽管我们可能观察到涌现能力在某个规模下发生，但该能力可能后来在更小的规模下实现——换句话说，模型规模并不是解锁涌现能力的唯一因素。随着训练大型语言模型的科学进步，某些能力可能会通过新的架构、更高质量的数据或改进的训练过程在更小的模型上解锁。例如，有14个BIG-Bench任务，LaMDA 137B和GPT-3 175B模型在这些任务上表现接近随机，但PaLM 62B实际上实现了高于随机的性能，尽管其模型参数和训练FLOPs更少。虽然没有实证研究消融PaLM 62B与先前模型之间的每个差异（计算成本太高），但PaLM性能更好的潜在原因可能包括高质量的训练数据（例如，比LaMDA更多的多语言和代码数据）和架构差异（例如，分割数字编码；参见Chowdhery等人（2022）第2节）。另一种可能解锁涌现的方式是通过不同的预训练目标——Tay等人（2022c）表明，在混合去噪器目标（Tay等人，2022a）上进行计算高效的持续预训练阶段，使得在几个BIG-Bench任务上实现了涌现性能。

items.en: Moreover, once an ability is discovered, further research may make the ability available for smaller scale models. Consider the nascent direction of enabling language models to follow natural language instructions describing a task (Wei et al., 2022a; Sanh et al., 2022; Ouyang et al., 2022, inter alia). Although Wei et al. (2022a) initially found that instruction-based finetuning only worked for 68B parameter or larger decoder-only models, Sanh et al. (2022) induced similar behavior in a 11B model with an encoder-decoder architecture, which typically has higher performance after finetuning than decoder-only architectures (Wang et al., 2022a). As another example, Ouyang et al. (2022) proposed a finetuning and reinforcement learning from human feedback approach for the InstructGPT models, which enabled a 1.3B model to outperform much larger models in human-rater evaluations on a broad set of use cases.
items.zh: 此外，一旦发现某种能力，进一步的研究可能使该能力对较小规模的模型可用。考虑使语言模型能够遵循描述任务的自然语言指令的新兴方向（Wei等人，2022a；Sanh等人，2022；Ouyang等人，2022等）。尽管Wei等人（2022a）最初发现基于指令的微调仅对68B参数或更大的仅解码器模型有效，但Sanh等人（2022）在具有编码器-解码器架构的11B模型中诱导了类似行为，该架构在微调后通常比仅解码器架构具有更高的性能（Wang等人，2022a）。作为另一个例子，Ouyang等人（2022）为InstructGPT模型提出了一种微调和基于人类反馈的强化学习方法，这使得1.3B模型在广泛用例的人工评分评估中优于更大的模型。

items.en: There has also been work on improving the general few-shot prompting abilities of language models (Gao et al., 2021; Schick & Schütze, 2021, inter alia). Theoretical and interpretability research (Wei et al., 2021a; Saunshi et al., 2021) on why a language modeling objective facilitates certain downstream behavior could in turn have implications on how to enable emergence beyond simply scaling. For instance, certain features of pre-training data (e.g., long-range coherence, having many rare classes) have also been shown to correlate with emergent few-shot prompting and could potentially enable it in smaller models (Xie et al., 2022; Chan et al., 2022), and few-shot learning can require certain model architectures in some scenarios (Chan et al., 2022). Computational linguistics work has further shown how threshold frequencies of training data can activate emergent syntactic rule-learning when model parameters and training FLOPs are held constant (Wei et al., 2021b), which has even been shown to have striking “aha” moments similar to those in the psycholinguistics literature (Abend et al., 2017; Zhang et al., 2021). As we continue to train language models, lowering the scale threshold for emergent abilities will become more important for making research on such abilities to available to the community more broadly (Bommasani et al., 2021; Ganguli et al., 2022; Liang et al., 2022).
items.zh: 还有关于提高语言模型通用少样本提示能力的工作（Gao等人，2021；Schick & Schütze，2021等）。关于语言建模目标为何促进某些下游行为的理论和可解释性研究（Wei等人，2021a；Saunshi等人，2021）可能反过来对如何超越简单扩展来启用涌现产生影响。例如，预训练数据的某些特征（例如，长程连贯性、具有许多稀有类别）已被证明与涌现的少样本提示相关，并可能使其在较小的模型中实现（Xie等人，2022；Chan等人，2022），并且在某些场景下，少样本学习可能需要特定的模型架构（Chan等人，2022）。计算语言学工作进一步表明，当模型参数和训练FLOPs保持不变时，训练数据的阈值频率如何激活涌现的句法规则学习（Wei等人，2021b），这甚至被证明具有与心理语言学文献中类似的惊人“啊哈”时刻（Abend等人，2017；Zhang等人，2021）。随着我们继续训练语言模型，降低涌现能力的规模阈值对于使此类能力的研究更广泛地惠及社区将变得更加重要（Bommasani等人，2021；Ganguli等人，2022；Liang等人，2022）。

items.en: Naturally, there are limitations to a program consisting only of increasing scale (training compute, model parameters, and dataset size). For instance, scaling may eventually be bottle-necked by hardware constraints, and some abilities may not have emerged at this point. Other abilities may never emerge—for instance, tasks that are far out of the distribution of even a very large training dataset might not ever achieve any significant performance. Finally, an ability could emerge and then plateau; in other words, there is no guarantee that scaling enables an ability to reach the desired level.
items.zh: 自然，仅由增加规模（训练算力、模型参数和数据集大小）组成的程序存在局限性。例如，扩展最终可能受到硬件限制，并且某些能力可能在此阶段尚未涌现。其他能力可能永远不会涌现——例如，即使非常大的训练数据集也远超出分布的任务可能永远无法达到任何显著性能。最后，一种能力可能涌现然后停滞；换句话说，不能保证扩展能使能力达到期望的水平。

items.en: While scale (e.g., training FLOPs or model parameters) has been highly correlated with language model performance on many downstream metrics so far, scale need not be the only lens to view emergent abilities. For example, the emergence of task-specific abilities can be analyzed as a function of the language model’s perplexity on a general text corpus such as WikiText103 (Merity et al., 2016). Figure 4 shows such a plot with WikiText103 perplexity of the language model on the x𝑥x-axis and performance on the MMLU benchmark on the y𝑦y-axis, side-by-side with plots of training FLOPs and model parameters on the x𝑥x-axis.
items.zh: 尽管规模（例如，训练FLOPs或模型参数）迄今为止与许多下游指标上的语言模型性能高度相关，但规模不必是看待涌现能力的唯一视角。例如，任务特定能力的涌现可以分析为语言模型在通用文本语料库（如WikiText103（Merity等人，2016））上的困惑度的函数。图4显示了这样一个图，x轴为语言模型的WikiText103困惑度，y轴为MMLU基准上的性能，旁边还有x轴为训练FLOPs和模型参数的图。

items.en: Because WikiText103 perplexity and training FLOPs happen to be highly correlated for the models considered here (Gopher and Chinchilla), the plots of emergent abilities look similar for both. However, this correlation between WikiText103 perplexity and scale may not hold in the future as new techniques beyond vanilla dense Transformer models are developed (e.g., retrieval-augmented models may have strong WikiText103 perplexity with less training compute and fewer model parameters (Borgeaud et al., 2021)). Also note that using WikiText103 perplexity to compare across model families can be complicated due to factors such as differences in training data composition. Overall, emergent abilities should probably be viewed as a function of many correlated variables.
items.zh: 由于对于这里考虑的模型（Gopher和Chinchilla），WikiText103困惑度和训练FLOPs恰好高度相关，因此涌现能力的图看起来相似。然而，随着超越普通密集Transformer模型的新技术（例如，检索增强模型可能以更少的训练算力和更少的模型参数获得强大的WikiText103困惑度（Borgeaud等人，2021））的发展，WikiText103困惑度与规模之间的这种相关性可能在未来不成立。还要注意，由于训练数据组成等因素的差异，使用WikiText103困惑度跨模型家族进行比较可能很复杂。总体而言，涌现能力可能应被视为许多相关变量的函数。

items.en: Importantly, similar to how emergent abilities have been observed in the few-shot prompting setting without explicitly being included in pre-training, risks could also emerge (Bommasani et al., 2021; Steinhardt, 2021; Ganguli et al., 2022). For instance, societal risks of large language models such as truthfulness, bias, and toxicity are a growing area of research (Weidinger et al., 2021). Such risks are important considerations whether or not they can be precisely characterized as “emergent” based on the definition in § 2, and, in some scenarios, do increase with model scale (see the Inverse Scaling Prize666https://github.com/inverse-scaling/prize). Since work on emergent abilities incentivizes scaling language models, it is important to be aware of risks that increase with model scale even if they are not emergent.
items.zh: 重要的是，类似于在少样本提示设置中观察到涌现能力而无需明确包含在预训练中，风险也可能涌现（Bommasani等人，2021；Steinhardt，2021；Ganguli等人，2022）。例如，大型语言模型的社会风险，如真实性、偏见和毒性，是一个日益增长的研究领域（Weidinger等人，2021）。无论这些风险是否可以根据§2中的定义精确地描述为“涌现”，它们都是重要的考虑因素，并且在某些情况下，确实随着模型规模的增加而增加（参见逆缩放奖）。由于关于涌现能力的工作激励了语言模型的扩展，因此即使风险不是涌现的，也要意识到随模型规模增加的风险是很重要的。

items.en: Here, we summarize several prior findings on the relationship between specific social risks and model scale. On WinoGender (Rudinger et al., 2017), which measures gender bias in occupations such as “nurse” or “electrician,” scaling has improved performance so far (Du et al., 2021; Chowdhery et al., 2022), though BIG-Bench (2022) found in BBQ bias benchmark (Parrish et al., 2022) that bias can increase with scaling for ambiguous contexts. As for toxicity, Askell et al. (2021) found that while larger language models could produce more toxic responses from the RealToxicityPrompts dataset (Gehman et al., 2020), this behavior could be mitigated by giving models prompts with examples of being “helpful, harmless, and honest.” For extracting training data from language models, larger models were found to be more likely to memorize training data (Carlini et al., 2021; 2022), though deduplication methods have been proposed and can simultaneously reduce memorization while improving performance (Kandpal et al., 2022; Lee et al., 2022a). The TruthfulQA benchmark (Lin et al., 2021) showed that GPT-3 models were more likely to mimic human falsehoods as they got larger, though Rae et al. (2021) later showed on a multiple-choice version that scaling Gopher to 280B enabled emergent performance substantially better than random.
items.zh: 在这里，我们总结了关于特定社会风险与模型规模之间关系的几个先前发现。在衡量职业（如“护士”或“电工”）中性别偏见的WinoGender（Rudinger等人，2017）上，扩展迄今为止提高了性能（Du等人，2021；Chowdhery等人，2022），尽管BIG-Bench（2022）在BBQ偏见基准（Parrish等人，2022）中发现，对于模糊上下文，偏见可能随扩展而增加。至于毒性，Askell等人（2021）发现，虽然较大的语言模型可能从RealToxicityPrompts数据集（Gehman等人，2020）产生更多有毒响应，但通过给模型提供“有帮助、无害和诚实”的示例提示可以缓解这种行为。对于从语言模型中提取训练数据，发现较大的模型更可能记忆训练数据（Carlini等人，2021；2022），尽管已经提出了去重方法，可以同时减少记忆并提高性能（Kandpal等人，2022；Lee等人，2022a）。TruthfulQA基准（Lin等人，2021）表明，GPT-3模型随着规模增大更可能模仿人类的虚假信息，尽管Rae等人（2021）后来在多项选择版本上表明，将Gopher扩展到280B使得涌现性能显著优于随机。

items.en: Beyond the above, emergent risks also include phenomena that might only exist in future language models or that have not yet been characterized in current language models. Some such behaviors, as discussed in detail in Hendrycks et al. (2021b), could be backdoor vulnerabilities, inadvertent deception, or harmful content synthesis. Approaches involving data filtering, forecasting, governance, and automatically discovering harmful behaviors have been proposed for discovering and mitigating emergent risks (Bender et al., 2021; Weidinger et al., 2021; Steinhardt, 2021; Ganguli et al., 2022; Perez et al., 2022, inter alia). For a more detailed discussion of the risks of large language models, including emergent risks, see Bender et al. (2021); Steinhardt (2021); Bommasani et al. (2021); Ganguli et al. (2022).
items.zh: 除了上述之外，涌现风险还包括可能仅存在于未来语言模型中或尚未在当前语言模型中被表征的现象。Hendrycks等人（2021b）详细讨论的一些此类行为可能是后门漏洞、无意欺骗或有害内容合成。已经提出了涉及数据过滤、预测、治理和自动发现有害行为的方法来发现和缓解涌现风险（Bender等人，2021；Weidinger等人，2021；Steinhardt，2021；Ganguli等人，2022；Perez等人，2022等）。关于大型语言模型风险（包括涌现风险）的更详细讨论，请参见Bender等人（2021）；Steinhardt（2021）；Bommasani等人（2021）；Ganguli等人（2022）。

items.en: Finally, the emergent abilities discussed here focus on model behavior and are just one of several types of emergence in NLP (Manning et al., 2020; Teehan et al., 2022). Another notable type of qualitative change is sociological, in which increasing scale has shifted how the community views and uses language models. For instance, NLP has historically focused on task-specific models (Jurafsky & Martin, 2009). Recently, scaling has led to an explosion in research on and development of models that are “general purpose” in that they are single models that aim to perform a range of tasks not explicitly encoded in the training data (e.g., GPT-3, Chinchilla, and PaLM) (Manning, 2022).
items.zh: 最后，这里讨论的涌现能力侧重于模型行为，只是NLP中几种涌现类型之一（Manning等人，2020；Teehan等人，2022）。另一种值得注意的定性变化是社会学的，其中规模的增加改变了社区看待和使用语言模型的方式。例如，NLP历史上专注于任务特定模型（Jurafsky & Martin，2009）。最近，扩展导致了研究和开发“通用”模型的爆炸式增长，这些模型是旨在执行一系列未明确编码在训练数据中的任务的单一模型（例如，GPT-3、Chinchilla和PaLM）（Manning，2022）。

items.en: One key set of results in the emergent sociological shift towards general-purpose models is when scaling enables a few-shot prompted general-purpose model to outperform prior state of the art held by finetuned task-specific models. As a few examples, GPT-3 175B achieved new state of the art on the TriviaQA and PiQA question-answering benchmarks (Brown et al., 2020); PaLM 540B achieved new state of the art on three arithmetic reasoning benchmarks (Chowdhery et al., 2022); and the multimodal Flamingo 80B model achieved new state of the art on six visual question answering benchmarks (Alayrac et al., 2022). In all of these cases, state-of-the-art performance was achieved by few-shot prompting a language model of unprecendented scale (scaling curves for these examples are shown in Appendix Figure 13). These abilities are not necessarily emergent since they have smooth, predictable scaling curves—however, they do underscore an emergent sociological shift towards general-purpose models in the NLP community.
items.zh: 在向通用模型的社会学转变中，一个关键的结果集是当扩展使得少样本提示的通用模型能够超越先前由微调的任务特定模型保持的最优性能时。举几个例子：GPT-3 175B在TriviaQA和PiQA问答基准上取得了新的最优性能（Brown等人，2020）；PaLM 540B在三个算术推理基准上取得了新的最优性能（Chowdhery等人，2022）；多模态Flamingo 80B模型在六个视觉问答基准上取得了新的最优性能（Alayrac等人，2022）。在所有这些情况下，最优性能是通过对空前规模的模型进行少样本提示实现的（这些例子的缩放曲线见附录图13）。这些能力不一定是涌现的，因为它们具有平滑、可预测的缩放曲线——然而，它们确实强调了NLP社区向通用模型的社会学转变。

items.en: The ability for general-purpose models to perform unseen tasks given only a few examples has also led to many new applications of language models outside the NLP research community. For instance, language models have been used via prompting to translate natural language instructions into actions executable by robots (Ahn et al., 2022; Huang et al., 2022), interact with users (Coenen et al., 2021; Wu et al., 2021; 2022a; Lee et al., 2022b), and facilitate multi-modal reasoning (Zeng et al., 2022; Alayrac et al., 2022). Large language models have also been deployed in the real-world both in products, such as GitHub CoPilot,777https://copilot.github.com/ and directly as services themselves, such as OpenAI’s GPT-3 API.888https://beta.openai.com/docs/introduction
items.zh: 通用模型仅凭少量示例就能执行未见任务的能力，也催生了NLP研究社区之外语言模型的许多新应用。例如，语言模型已通过提示被用于将自然语言指令翻译成机器人可执行的动作（Ahn等人，2022；Huang等人，2022）、与用户交互（Coenen等人，2021；Wu等人，2021；2022a；Lee等人，2022b），以及促进多模态推理（Zeng等人，2022；Alayrac等人，2022）。大型语言模型也已部署到现实世界中，既体现在产品中，如GitHub CoPilot，也直接作为服务本身，如OpenAI的GPT-3 API。

items.en: Future work on emergent abilities could involve train more-capable language models, as well as methods for better enabling language models to perform tasks. Some potential directions include but are not limited to the following.
items.zh: 关于涌现能力的未来工作可能涉及训练更强大的语言模型，以及更好地使语言模型执行任务的方法。一些潜在方向包括但不限于以下内容。

items.en: Further model scaling. Further scaling up models has so far appeared to increase the capabilities of language models, and is a straightforward direction for future work. However, simply scaling up language models is computationally expensive and requires solving substantial hardware challenges, and so other approaches will likely play a key role in the future of the emergent abilities of large language models.
items.zh: 进一步模型Scaling。迄今为止，进一步扩大模型规模似乎增强了语言模型的能力，这是一个直接的未来工作方向。然而，简单地扩大语言模型计算成本高昂，且需要解决重大的硬件挑战，因此其他方法可能在未来大型语言模型涌现能力中发挥关键作用。

items.en: Improved model architectures and training. Improving model architecture and training procedures may facilitate high-quality models with emergent abilities while mitigating computational cost. One direction is using sparse mixture-of-experts architectures (Lepikhin et al., 2021; Fedus et al., 2021; Artetxe et al., 2021; Zoph et al., 2022), which scale up the number of parameters in a model while maintaining constant computational costs for an input. Other directions for better computational efficiency could involve variable amounts of compute for different inputs (Graves, 2016; Dehghani et al., 2018), using more localized learning strategies than backpropagation through all weights in a neural network (Jaderberg et al., 2017), and augmenting models with external memory (Guu et al., 2020; Borgeaud et al., 2021; Wu et al., 2022b, inter alia). These nascent directions have already shown promise in many settings but have not yet seen widespread adoption, which will likely require further work.
items.zh: 改进的模型架构和训练。改进模型架构和训练过程可能有助于在降低计算成本的同时，获得具有涌现能力的高质量模型。一个方向是使用稀疏混合专家架构（Lepikhin等人，2021；Fedus等人，2021；Artetxe等人，2021；Zoph等人，2022），该架构在保持输入计算成本恒定的同时，扩展模型参数数量。其他提高计算效率的方向可能包括对不同输入使用可变计算量（Graves，2016；Dehghani等人，2018），使用比通过神经网络所有权重反向传播更局部的学习策略（Jaderberg等人，2017），以及用外部存储器增强模型（Guu等人，2020；Borgeaud等人，2021；Wu等人，2022b，等）。这些新兴方向已在许多场景中显示出前景，但尚未得到广泛采用，这可能需要进一步的工作。

items.en: Data scaling. Training long enough on a large-enough dataset has been shown to be key for the ability of language models to acquire syntactic, semantic, and other world knowledge (Zhang et al., 2021; Wei et al., 2021b; Razeghi et al., 2022). Recently, Hoffmann et al. (2022) argued that prior work (Kaplan et al., 2020) underestimated the amount of training data needed to train a compute-optimal model, underscoring the importance of training data. Collecting large datasets so that models can be trained for longer could allow a greater range of emergent abilities under a fixed model size constraint.
items.zh: 数据Scaling。在足够大的数据集上训练足够长的时间，已被证明是语言模型获取句法、语义和其他世界知识的关键（Zhang等人，2021；Wei等人，2021b；Razeghi等人，2022）。最近，Hoffmann等人（2022）认为，先前的工作（Kaplan等人，2020）低估了训练计算最优模型所需的训练数据量，强调了训练数据的重要性。收集大型数据集以便模型能够训练更长时间，可以在固定模型大小约束下实现更广泛的涌现能力。

items.en: Better techniques for and understanding of prompting. Although few-shot prompting (Brown et al., 2020) is simple and effective, general improvements to prompting may further expand the abilities of language models. For instance, simple modifications such as calibrating output probabilities (Zhao et al., 2021; Holtzman et al., 2021) or using a noisy channel (Min et al., 2022a) have improved performance on a range of tasks. Augmenting few-shot exemplars with intermediate steps (Reynolds & McDonell, 2021; Nye et al., 2021; Wei et al., 2022b) has also enabled models to perform multi-step reasoning tasks not possible in the standard prompting formulation from Brown et al. (2020). Moreover, better exploration of what makes prompting successful (Wei et al., 2021a; Xie et al., 2022; Min et al., 2022b; Olsson et al., 2022) could lead to insights on how to elicit emergent abilities at a smaller model scale. Sufficient understanding of why models work generally lags the development and popularization of techniques such as few-shot prompting, and it is also likely that the best practices for prompting will change as more-powerful models are developed over time.
items.zh: 更好的提示技术和理解。尽管少样本提示（Brown等人，2020）简单有效，但对提示的通用改进可能进一步扩展语言模型的能力。例如，简单的修改如校准输出概率（Zhao等人，2021；Holtzman等人，2021）或使用噪声通道（Min等人，2022a）已在一系列任务上提升了性能。用中间步骤增强少样本示例（Reynolds & McDonell，2021；Nye等人，2021；Wei等人，2022b）也使模型能够执行Brown等人（2020）标准提示公式中无法实现的多步推理任务。此外，更好地探索提示成功的原因（Wei等人，2021a；Xie等人，2022；Min等人，2022b；Olsson等人，2022）可能带来如何在较小模型规模下激发涌现能力的见解。对模型工作原理的充分理解通常滞后于少样本提示等技术的发展和普及，并且随着更强大模型的开发，提示的最佳实践也可能随时间变化。

items.en: Frontier tasks. Although language models can perform a wide range of tasks, there are still many tasks that even the largest language models to date cannot perform with above-random accuracy. Dozens of such tasks from BIG-Bench are enumerated in § E.4; these tasks often involve abstract reasoning (e.g., playing Chess, challenging math, etc). Future research could potentially investigate why these abilities have not yet emerged, and how to enable models to perform these tasks. Looking forward, another growing direction could be multilingual emergence; results on multilingual BIG-Bench tasks indicate that both model scale and training data play a role in emergence (e.g., Figure 2D shows that both using PaLM’s training dataset and scaling to 62B parameters is required for question-answering in Persian). Other frontier tasks could include prompting in multiple modalities (Alayrac et al., 2022; Ramesh et al., 2022).
items.zh: 前沿任务。尽管语言模型可以执行广泛的任务，但仍有许多任务即使是迄今为止最大的语言模型也无法以高于随机的准确率执行。BIG-Bench中列举了数十个这样的任务（见§ E.4）；这些任务通常涉及抽象推理（例如下棋、挑战性数学等）。未来的研究可能探究这些能力为何尚未涌现，以及如何使模型能够执行这些任务。展望未来，另一个增长方向可能是多语言涌现；多语言BIG-Bench任务的结果表明，模型规模和训练数据都在涌现中发挥作用（例如，图2D显示，使用PaLM的训练数据集并扩展到62B参数对于波斯语问答是必要的）。其他前沿任务可能包括多模态提示（Alayrac等人，2022；Ramesh等人，2022）。

items.en: Understanding emergence. Beyond research on unlocking further emergence, an open question for future research is how and why emergent abilities occur in large language models. This paper conducted initial analyses regarding scaling of the cross-entropy loss on BIG-Bench (§ A.1), different metrics for generative tasks (§ A.2), and which types of tasks emergence occurs (§ A.3 and Appendix B). These analyses did not provide complete answers to why emergence occurs or how to predict it. Future research could potentially analyze emergence in new ways (e.g., analyze the relationship between emergent tasks and similar data in training; create a synthetic task that requires multiple compositional sub-tasks and evaluate how each of those sub-tasks improve with scale and unlock emergence when combined). Overall, understanding emergence is an important direction because it could potentially allow us predict what abilities future models may have, as well as provide new insights into how to train more-capable language models.
items.zh: 理解涌现。除了解锁进一步涌现的研究之外，未来研究的一个开放问题是涌现能力如何以及为何在大型语言模型中出现。本文对BIG-Bench上的交叉熵损失Scaling（§ A.1）、生成任务的不同指标（§ A.2）以及涌现发生的任务类型（§ A.3和附录B）进行了初步分析。这些分析并未提供涌现为何发生或如何预测的完整答案。未来的研究可能以新的方式分析涌现（例如，分析涌现任务与训练中相似数据的关系；创建一个需要多个组合子任务的合成任务，并评估每个子任务如何随规模改进并在组合时解锁涌现）。总体而言，理解涌现是一个重要方向，因为它可能使我们能够预测未来模型可能具备的能力，并为如何训练更强大的语言模型提供新见解。

items.en: We have discussed emergent abilities of language models, for which meaningful performance has only been thus far observed at a certain computational scale. Emergent abilities can span a variety of language models, task types, and experimental scenarios. Such abilities are a recently discovered outcome of scaling up language models, and the questions of how they emerge and whether more scaling will enable further emergent abilities seem to be important future research directions for the field of NLP.
items.zh: 我们讨论了语言模型的涌现能力，这些能力迄今为止仅在特定计算规模下观察到有意义的性能。涌现能力可以涵盖各种语言模型、任务类型和实验场景。这些能力是扩大语言模型规模的新近发现结果，它们如何涌现以及更多Scaling是否会带来进一步的涌现能力，似乎是NLP领域重要的未来研究方向。

items.en: In this paper, we surveyed results in the existing literature, without proposing new methods or models. As discussed in (§ 5), emergent abilities are unpredictable in several ways, and include emergent risks (§ 5.4). We believe these phenomena warrant careful study and raise important questions for the field.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们调查了现有文献中的结果，没有提出新的方法或模型。如（§ 5）所述，涌现能力在多个方面不可预测，并包括涌现风险（§ 5.4）。我们认为这些现象值得仔细研究，并为该领域提出了重要问题。

items.en: We thank Charles Sutton, Slav Petrov, Douglas Eck, Jason Freidenfelds, Jascha Sohl-Dickstein, Ethan Dyer, Dale Schuurmans, and Xavier Garcia for useful discussions and feedback on the manuscript.
items.zh: 我们感谢Charles Sutton、Slav Petrov、Douglas Eck、Jason Freidenfelds、Jascha Sohl-Dickstein、Ethan Dyer、Dale Schuurmans和Xavier Garcia对本文的有益讨论和反馈。
